check.name: JavaNCSS
check.description:
Determines complexity of methods, classes and files by counting the Non Commenting Source Statements (NCSS). This check adheres to the specification for the JavaNCSS-Tool written by Chr. Clemens Lee.

Roughly said the NCSS metric is calculated by counting the source lines which are not comments, (nearly) equivalent to counting the semicolons and opening curly braces.

The NCSS for a class is summarized from the NCSS of all its methods, the NCSS of its nested classes and the number of member variable declarations.

The NCSS for a file is summarized from the ncss of all its top level classes, the number of imports and the package declaration.

Rationale: Too large methods and classes are hard to read and costly to maintain. A large NCSS number often means that a method or class has too many responsibilities and/or functionalities which should be decomposed into smaller units.

Here is a breakdown of what exactly is counted and not counted:

JavaNCSS metrics
StructureNCSS CountNotes
Package declaration1 Counted at the terminating semicolon.
Import declaration1 Each single, static, or wildcard import counts as 1.
Class, Interface, Annotation (@interface)1 Counted at the opening curly brace of the body.
Method, Constructor1 Counted at the declaration.
Static initializer, Instance initializer1 Both static {} and bare {} initializer blocks count as 1.
Annotation type member1 Each method-like member declaration inside @interface counts as 1. A standalone ; inside @interface also counts as 1.
Variable declaration1 1 per statement regardless of how many variables are declared on that line. int x, y; counts as 1.
if1 The if keyword counts as 1.
else, else if1 The else keyword counts as 1, separate from the if count.
while, do, for1 The keyword header counts as 1.
switch1 The switch keyword counts as 1.
case, default1 Every case and default label adds 1.
try0 The try keyword itself does not count.
catch1 Each catch block counts as 1.
finally1 The finally block counts as 1.
synchronized1 The synchronized statement counts as 1.
return, break, continue, throw1 Each counts as 1.
assert1 Each assert statement counts as 1, with or without a message expression.
Labeled statement1 label: statement counts as 1.
Explicit constructor invocation1 this() or super() calls inside a constructor body each count as 1.
Expression statements (assignments, method calls)1 Statement-level expressions terminated by ; count as 1. A method call inside a return does not add an extra count.
Empty blocks {}0 Empty curly braces do not increase the count.
Empty statements ;0 Standalone semicolons outside of @interface do not increase the count.

property.name: classMaximum
property.description: Specify the maximum allowed number of non commenting lines in a class.

property.name: fileMaximum
property.description: Specify the maximum allowed number of non commenting lines in a file including all top level and nested classes.

property.name: methodMaximum
property.description: Specify the maximum allowed number of non commenting lines in a method.

property.name: recordMaximum
property.description: Specify the maximum allowed number of non commenting lines in a record.
